{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query specifically mentions 'beveling of the pulpoaxial line angle of a class II cavity'. This terminology is related to the anatomy of the tooth and the way a restoration is prepared. Beveling the line angle is typically done to reduce stress concentrations and prevent fractures. Since class II cavities involve both the occlusal and axial surfaces, beveling the line angle helps distribute occlusal forces more evenly and reduces the risk of fracture.
